grid.rationale: Almost no domestic high-purity silicon, photoresist, specialty gases or slurries (>90% imported); gallium China-controlled. [orfonline.org semiconductor supply chain]
No electronic-grade silicon/chemical refining at scale; SPECS localisation pre-production mid-2026. [orfonline.org]
Emerging SiC (SiCSem), GaN (Crystal Matrix), discretes (CDIL), SCL 180nm — but most wafer/substrate inputs imported. [PIB PRID 2155459]
Total import dependency on ASML, Applied Materials/Lam, Tokyo Electron; no domestic WFE maker. [asml.com Tata partnership]
Two OSATs operational (Micron, Kaynes), more ramping, first logic megafab ~50% built — but process IP licensed from PSMC/Renesas. [tataelectronics.com]
Emerging design layer (DLI 24 projects/16 tape-outs, C-DAC ChipIN) but leading-edge fab outsourced to TSMC, EDA/IP foreign. [digitimes.com]
Reserves exist (REE 6.9 Mt, graphite 8.6 Mt) but bankable extraction thin: Reasi auction failed twice, lithium ~100% imported. [business-standard.com Reasi]
Weakest layer: ~100% China-dependent refined NdFeB, ~98% gallium, ~90% battery graphite; NALCO gallium pre-commercial. [anderseninstitute.org]
Finished sintered NdFeB magnets ~100% imported (53,748 t FY25); domestic chain absent until REPM units commission. [mining-technology.com]
Magnet sintering/REE separation/gallium-extraction equipment import-dependent; REPM explicitly subsidises capex. [pmindia.gov.in REPM]
IREL is sole integrated monazite-to-magnet operator; recyclers (Lohum, Attero) close the loop, but no integrated chain at scale yet. [irel.co.in]
Strongest layer: GSI/MECL exploration, NALCO-BARC gallium tech, Lohum patented hydromet (90%+ recovery). [mecl.co.in]
Captive renewables exist (Reliance, Adani) but transmission evacuation strands power; grid not built for AI racks. [nextias.com DC energy]
Zero advanced-node capability; Dholera fabs 28nm+ vs 3-5nm AI silicon; all AI chips imported and TSMC-CoWoS-bound. [indmoney.com semiconductor gap]
Total import dependency on Nvidia (~80% share) with AMD a marginal hedge; the sovereignty break point. [introl.com GPU landscape]
Indian firms build shells and own subsea cables, but networking silicon, switches, optics overwhelmingly imported. [wikipedia DC industry India]
Strong, largely domestically operated: Reliance, AdaniConneX, CtrlS, Yotta, Nxtra run GW-scale builds + IndiaAI cluster. [PIB PRID 2132817]
Genuine indigenous foundation-model capability (Sarvam 105B, Krutrim) but trained on imported GPUs; MLOps AWS/Azure/Google-dominated. [business-standard.com Sarvam]
MIDHANI sole domestic source of titanium/maraging steel but still seeks JVs for overseas superalloy feedstock. [tipranks.com MIDHANI]
MIDHANI indigenised three master alloys, gained CEMILAC/NADCAP certs, raised titanium output ~40%; high-end melt maturing. [tipranks.com MIDHANI]
Hard chokepoint: jet engines wholly import-dependent (GE F404/F414), GaN/SiC at lab scale, AIP from TKMS, S-400 from Russia. [business-standard.com HAL-GE]
Machine tools, fab/clean-room and high-end test infra largely imported; DRDO SSPL/GAETEC exist but base foreign-reliant. [theweek.in DRDO GaN]
Platform assembly strongly indigenous: Tejas, ATAGS, Akash/BrahMos, Vikrant, K9; FY25 production Rs 1.54 lakh cr, exports Rs 23,622 cr. [PIB PRID 2154551]
Design IP held domestically (DRDO/ADA: Tejas, AMCA, Pinaka) but source-code/critical-tech ToT for imported platforms withheld. [theprint.in AMCA]
EDI 81.7 (Captured). AWS/Azure/GCP dominate; CLOUD Act exposure. No domestic hyperscaler.
EDI 78.5 (Captured). SAP/Oracle at the transactional core of PSUs and large industry; Ramco the lone domestic top-tier player.
EDI 72.5. M365/Workspace near-total; negligible domestic alternative.
EDI 70.8. Windows endpoint monopoly.
EDI 70.0. Oracle/SQL Server dominate, but PostgreSQL is a credible sovereign exit (nascent in enterprise).
EDI 69.2. Frontier models foreign-led; open-weight + Indian LLM efforts nascent.
EDI 63.2. Foreign-led; domestic security product base nascent.
EDI 56.0. Zoho and Freshworks make this the one application layer with real domestic products (emerging).
EDI 45.5. Linux/open-source — substantial sovereignty; India consumes freely, contributes less.
EDI 42.6. Aadhaar — state-built, sovereign by construction.
EDI 37.4. UPI — open-standards sovereign rails; world reference DPI.

events.title: SAP ECC end-of-support (Dec 2027) forces re-platform decision on 5,000+ Indian enterprises
events.summary: SAP ends ECC mainstream maintenance 31 Dec 2027 (extended to 2030 at +2%/yr; private-edition option to 2033). End-2024 only ~39% (14,000/35,000) of ECC customers had moved to S/4HANA; ~17,000 holdouts projected at deadline. Migrations take 18-42 months → 2026

events.title: [PUBLISHED·PAID] The Opportunity Beyond the Fab
events.summary: Fab-adjacent opportunity surface (existing folder).

events.title: Sovereign-software opportunity sized: ~$51bn TAM by 2035, $4.6-10.2bn/yr import-replacement
events.summary: Modelled: India addressable enterprise-software + cloud market ~USD 169bn by 2035; sovereign-capturable USD 34-68bn (central ~51bn). Replaceable foreign-software spend ~USD 4.6-10.2bn/yr (central ~7.4bn) +40-60% saving. Export models: Israel-style deep-tech pr

events.title: DIEM scenarios 2026-2035: outcome ranges from 71% captured (BAU) to 38% sovereign (AI Leapfrog)
events.summary: Modelled DIEM run forward under 5 scenarios from ~59% (2026): A Business as Usual rises to 71% (doing nothing = deeper capture); B Managed Diversification 54%; C Sovereign Software Mission 43% (floored ~40 per China Xinchuang high-end ceiling); D AI Leapfrog 3

events.title: Strategic roadmap: four-actor moves keyed to the 2027 window
events.summary: Govt: time-bound Sovereign Software Mission (incumbents excluded, GAIA-X lesson; Xinchuang-calibrated ambition), Public-Money-Public-Code procurement, National ERP for PSUs, sovereign LLM as infrastructure, SBOM/zero-trust now, confront the cloud gap. Enterpri

events.title: [SERIES] Technology Sovereignty Series — V1 SAP (done), V2 Cloud, V3 AI, V4 Engineering SW, V5 Synthesis + Index 2027
events.summary: Techadyant research programme mapping India's technology-dependency layers on the EDI/SSS/DIEM frameworks, building incrementally toward the India Technology Sovereignty Index 2027. Plan: Reports and DPR/Technology-Sovereignty-Series-PLAN.md.

events.title: [V2 Cloud] Foreign hyperscaler dominance of India's cloud layer; no domestic hyperscaler at scale
events.summary: AWS/Azure/GCP control the public-cloud substrate beneath India's enterprise stack — the most Captured layer on the SAP Question EDI. Candidate signal for The Cloud Question (Volume 2). Needs India-specific market-share + capex quantification.

events.title: [V2 Cloud] US CLOUD Act extraterritorial reach over India data held on foreign hyperscalers
events.summary: The US CLOUD Act lets US authorities compel data from US-headquartered cloud providers regardless of where it is stored, intersecting India's DPDP/data-residency regime. Candidate policy signal for The Cloud Question.

events.title: [V3 AI] Foreign control of India's enterprise AI / agent layer; sovereign-model gap
events.summary: OpenAI, Anthropic and Google dominate the frontier-model and emerging agent-infrastructure layer; IndiaAI/Sarvam are the sovereign alternative still emerging. A sovereign stack on a foreign model is no sovereignty. Candidate signal for The AI Question (Volume

events.title: [V4 Eng-SW] Foreign dominance of India's industrial & design software (Cadence, Synopsys, Ansys, CATIA, Siemens, PTC)
events.summary: The EDA + CAD/PLM/simulation layer that runs India's chip, aerospace, automotive and heavy-engineering design is almost entirely foreign-controlled and almost undiscussed. Cadence/Synopsys converge with the Sovereign-EDA opportunity (semiconductors corridor).

events.title: SAP at the operational core of Indian industry
events.summary: >5,000 Indian enterprises run SAP; SAP India Pvt Ltd revenue ~Rs 8,870 cr FY25 (~16% growth). Confirmed deployments span ONGC, IndianOil, BPCL, SAIL, NTPC, BHEL, BSNL.

events.title: NITI Aayog publishes 'Future of India''s Semiconductor Industry' (Vision 2035)
events.summary: Govt roadmap: target 10-13% of global market, USD 120-150bn value chain, value retention 55-70% by 2035; strategic choice to win chokepoints (advanced packaging/OSAT top-3, SiC/GaN wide-bandgap, sovereign EDA/IP) rather than the wafer race. Research-opportunit

events.title: ISM 2.0 announced in Union Budget 2026 — shift to ecosystem deepening
events.summary: India Semiconductor Mission 2.0 sharpens focus on advanced packaging, compound semiconductors, critical design infrastructure and long-term capital. Signals move from ecosystem initiation to scale-up.

events.title: Agentic AI reset re-prices the enterprise-software stack
events.summary: SAP Joule (Joule Studio GA Q1 2026) and Microsoft Copilot embed agents across ERP/productivity; Gartner expects 40% of enterprise apps to embed task-specific AI agents by end-2026 (from <5% in 2025). The reset both deepens incumbent lock-in and collapses build

events.title: Zoho launches Zoho ERP (Jan 2026) — first credible Indian ERP shot
events.summary: Zoho launched Zoho ERP from Kumbakonam, Tamil Nadu in Jan 2026 — the most credible Indian software firm entering the exact category where dependency is deepest. Arrives as the 2027 SAP ECC deadline forces a collective re-platform decision. First Indian ERP opt

events.title: Chip-design tool dependency: EDA Big-3 >70% of market; India's C-DAC EDA grid subsidises foreign access
events.summary: Synopsys ~31%, Cadence ~30%, Siemens ~13% (>70% combined). India's DLI/C-DAC national EDA grid (95 startups, ~5.4m tool-hours by Jan 2026) provides subsidised access to the foreign oligopoly, not a sovereign alternative; RISC-V (DIR-V/SHAKTI/VEGA) + open-sourc

events.title: IndiaAI Mission common compute crosses 34,000 GPUs (subsidised sovereign compute)
events.summary: IndiaAI Mission (approved Mar 2025; Rs 10,300 cr, doubling planned) onboarded a common compute facility crossing 34,000 and approaching 40,000 GPUs, subsidised to Indian startups and researchers. Removes the single largest barrier to building foundation models

events.title: Zoho displaces foreign productivity across India's central government
events.summary: Following a Sept-2023 tender, all ~1.2 million central-government employee email accounts migrated off the NIC system onto Zoho Mail by 2025; productivity moved to Zoho Workplace. The most significant documented case of an Indian product displacing foreign-equ

events.title: Sarvam selected to build India's sovereign LLM (open 120B-param)
events.summary: Under IndiaAI, Sarvam building an open 120B-parameter sovereign model (after Sarvam-1, Sarvam-M); ~Rs 246.72 cr support; SoketAI/Gan AI/Gnani AI + 12 shortlisted teams. A sovereign reasoning layer means an AI-native enterprise stack need not relocate dependenc

events.title: US ordered Synopsys, Cadence, Siemens to halt EDA sales to China (May 2025); lifted Jul 2025
events.summary: EDA proven sanctionable: BIS required licences for all EDA exports to China May 2025, rescinded Jul 2025 after trade truce. 'Software as a sanction' applied to chip design — the SAP Question mechanism. India in the same structural seat. Core signal for the Sov

events.title: Zoho FY25: Rs 12,313 cr revenue, Rs 3,191 cr profit, fully bootstrapped
events.summary: Zoho revenue Rs 12,313 cr FY25 (+18%, ~doubled from Rs 6,711 cr FY22), profit Rs 3,191 cr, never raised external capital. The existence proof that an Indian firm can build a broad, profitable enterprise-software suite vs US incumbents.

events.title: India's foreign IP/software payments near double to ~USD 16bn (2020→2024)
events.summary: RBI/World Bank BoP: India's charges for use of intellectual property (payments) rose from ~USD 7.2bn (2020) to ~USD 16.3bn (2024); receipts only ~USD 1.5bn → net outflow ~USD 14.5bn/yr. All-IP line (software a large, growing, unquantified share). Upper-bound p

events.title: CrowdStrike (Jul 2024): single faulty update crashes 8.5M systems
events.summary: A defective CrowdStrike Falcon update crashed ~8.5 million Windows systems worldwide — largest IT outage in history; 5,000+ flights cancelled; global losses >USD 10bn (Fortune 500 up to USD 5.4bn). No adversary. Calibrates cloud-disruption scenario (B): update

events.title: XZ Utils (2024): 2.6-year open-source backdoor (CVE-2024-3094)
events.summary: A threat actor spent 2.6 years gaining maintainer trust to backdoor liblzma (used by OpenSSH on most Linux servers); CVSS 10.0; caught by chance. Shows open source is not automatically safe — but is auditable (caught because the code was open). Foundational to

events.title: MOSIP: India exports its sovereign-identity stack to 9+ countries
events.summary: MOSIP (IIIT Bangalore, 2018; Aadhaar model) is the foundational ID system for Philippines, Ethiopia, Morocco (full), Togo/Sri Lanka/Uganda (advanced) + pilots; 100M+ IDs issued by 2023. India is a net EXPORTER of sovereign-identity software — the hinge proof t

events.title: ONGC migrates Project ICE to SAP S/4HANA (11 Dec 2022)
events.summary: ONGC confirmed (own channel) that its ICE/SAP system of record — 23 modules, 500+ locations, 14,000+ users since 2004 — upgraded to SAP S/4HANA on 11-Dec-2022. Confirms continued deep SAP dependency at the core of India's largest oil & gas producer, now on the

events.title: Russia 2022: foreign enterprise-software withdrawal as a strategic-shock precedent
events.summary: SAP, Oracle, Microsoft and IBM suspended Russian operations within days in Mar 2022; Oracle cut support/updates/patches. Microsoft had served up to 90% of Russian corporate/state clients. No domestic banking equivalents existed; Russian banks sought to delay m

events.title: SolarWinds (2020): update-channel supply-chain attack precedent
events.summary: Russian SVR injected malicious code into routine SolarWinds Orion updates reaching ~18,000 organisations; 9 US federal agencies deeply compromised; undetected ~9 months. Calibrates the cyber/supply-chain risk scenario (D): every foreign vendor's update channel

players.name: AdaniConneX Private Limited
players.description: Adani x EdgeConneX JV; ~$100bn AI-DC pledge anchored by Google (Visakhapatnam) and Microsoft.

players.name: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.
players.description: Only credible accelerator alternative to Nvidia (MI300X/MI350); empanelled under IndiaAI but a hedge, not an escape.

players.name: Aeronautical Development Agency
players.description: DRDO design house holding design authority for Tejas and AMCA — the IP node even where private primes build.

players.name: Amazon Web Services India
players.description: Largest hyperscaler footprint (Mumbai + Hyderabad); foreign-controlled compute most Indian AI workloads run on.

players.name: Applied Materials Inc.
players.description: World's largest wafer-fab-equipment maker; India fabs fully import-dependent on AMAT process tools.

players.name: ASML Holding NV
players.description: Global lithography monopoly; supplies DUV tools to Dholera. India's advanced-patterning depends entirely on ASML.

players.name: Attero Recycling Private Limited
players.description: Major e-waste/Li-ion recycler; patented hydromet black-mass processing recovering Li/Ni/Co/REE.

players.name: Bharat Dynamics Limited
players.description: Sole DPSU missile-integration prime — Akash, Astra, QRSAM, ATGMs; core node for guided-munitions stockpile.

players.name: Bharat Electronics Limited
players.description: Dominant defence-electronics prime — radar, EW, fire-control, SAM electronics; load-bearing for nearly every system.

players.name: Bharat Forge Limited (Kalyani Strategic Systems)
players.description: Private artillery/forgings prime; lead ATAGS supplier, AMCA consortium lead; controls gun-forging/barrel capacity.

players.name: Bharti Airtel Limited
players.description: Owns Nxtra and Mumbai/Chennai cable landings (2Africa Pearls, SEA-ME-WE-6); a subsea-landing chokepoint.

players.name: BrahMos Aerospace Private Limited
players.description: India-Russia supersonic cruise-missile JV; India's flagship strike-missile and export node.

players.name: CAMYEN SE
players.description: Catamarca state miner; KABIL's counterparty on India's first sovereign overseas lithium asset (5 blocks).

players.name: Centre for Development of Advanced Computing
players.description: MeitY R&D body; nodal agency for DLI and the cloud EDA/IP 'ChipIN' gateway for fabless startups.

players.name: CG Power and Industrial Solutions Limited
players.description: Murugappa/TII-owned promoter holding ~92.3% of CG Semi; domestic anchor of a Sanand OSAT.

players.name: CG Semi Private Limited
players.description: CG Power-led Sanand OSAT JV (CG/Renesas/Stars); ramping to 15M units/day.

players.name: Chargebee
players.description: Indian subscription-revenue-management SaaS; ~USD 3.5bn valuation. Chennai-origin.

players.name: Coal India Limited
players.description: Maharatna coal monopoly diversifying into critical minerals; pursuing Argentina lithium-brine assets.

players.name: Cochin Shipyard Limited
players.description: Builder of the indigenous aircraft carrier Vikrant; the only Indian yard proven on carrier integration.

players.name: Continental Device India Limited
players.description: India's oldest semiconductor maker; Mohali expansion for high-power discretes (MOSFETs, IGBTs, SiC).

players.name: Critical Minerals Facilitation Office (Australia)
players.description: Australian govt office; KABIL counterparty under the India-Australia Critical Minerals Investment Partnership.

players.name: Crystal Matrix Limited
players.description: Approved (2026) for India's first commercial Mini/Micro-LED GaN display + GaN foundry at Dholera.

players.name: CtrlS Datacenters Limited
players.description: Largest Indian-owned Rated-4 colocation operator; IndiaAI-empanelled domestic-controlled hyperscale capacity.

players.name: Darwinbox
players.description: Indian HR-tech SaaS unicorn; revenue ~Rs 393 cr FY24 (+58%). Domestic alternative to Workday/SAP HCM.

players.name: Dassault Aviation
players.description: Rafale OEM; 26 Rafale-Marine signed 2025; France now India's #2 arms supplier.

players.name: Defence Research and Development Organisation
players.description: Apex defence-design/IP authority (~50 labs); designs the systems DPSUs build; holds indigenous GaN/SiC breakthrough.

players.name: Defence Semiconductor / GaN-SiC Capability (chokepoint)
players.description: The sensor/EW chokepoint — GaN/SiC RF chips for radar/EW/missiles; DRDO cracked indigenous GaN at pilot scale in 2026.

players.name: Department of Defence Production
players.description: MoD department administering DPSUs, indigenisation lists and iDEX; owner of the production/export value targets.

players.name: Design Linked Incentive Scheme
players.description: Sole structured funding channel for India's fabless/IP layer; 24 projects, 16 tape-outs by 2026.

players.name: E2E Networks Limited
players.description: First IndiaAI provider to deploy 100% of committed GPUs; the proof-of-execution node for the GPU pillar.

players.name: Frappe Technologies (ERPNext)
players.description: Indian-built open-source ERP (GPLv3) on Frappe/MariaDB; GST & e-invoicing native; adopted by NDDB and state bodies.

players.name: Freshworks
players.description: Indian-origin CX/CRM SaaS; NASDAQ-listed, approaching US$1bn revenue.

players.name: Gallium
players.description: Critical input for GaN/compound-semi and RF/power devices; China controls ~98-99% with export controls.

players.name: Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers Limited
players.description: Frigate/corvette yard; L1 for 5 of 8 Next-Generation Corvettes; surface-combatant pipeline node.

players.name: GE Aerospace
players.description: THE jet-engine chokepoint — sole supplier of F404/F414 engines for Tejas; delays have throttled deliveries.

players.name: Geological Survey of India
players.description: National exploration agency; 1,200 NCMM exploration projects; the upstream resource-definition chokepoint.

players.name: Google Cloud India
players.description: $15bn AP project; Jamnagar region for Reliance + Visakhapatnam campus with Adani; equity in Jio.

players.name: Graphite (battery-anode grade)
players.description: Largest anode material by weight in Li-ion cells; China ~90% battery-grade refining with export curbs.

players.name: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited
players.description: Sole indigenous fighter/helicopter integrator; Tejas prime; the chokepoint linking airpower to foreign engines.

players.name: Hindustan Copper Limited
players.description: Sole domestic copper-ore miner of scale; chronic under-output leaves India dependent on imported concentrate.

players.name: Hindustan Zinc Limited
players.description: World's largest integrated zinc/silver producer; pivoting to multi-metal critical-mineral recovery.

players.name: Hon Hai Technology Group
players.description: World's largest contract manufacturer; JV partner in India Chip OSAT and broad India electronics build-out.

players.name: India Chip (HCL-Foxconn JV)
players.description: HCL-Foxconn display-driver OSAT at Jewar/YEIDA (UP), ~Rs 3,706 cr; extends OSAT beyond Gujarat.

players.name: India Electronics and Semiconductor Association
players.description: Apex ESDM industry body; convenes industry-government policy and SEMICON India inputs.

players.name: India Semiconductor Mission
players.description: Independent business division under MeitY; single approval/disbursement gate for every fab and OSAT.

players.name: India-Asia-Xpress / India-Europe-Xpress cable systems
players.description: Reliance/Jio-led subsea systems landing at Mumbai/Chennai; the international-bandwidth chokepoint for Indian inferencing.

players.name: IndiaAI Mission
players.description: Rs 10,372 cr scheme; Compute pillar subsidises a pooled GPU cluster (>38,000 GPUs) — the compute demand-aggregation chokepoint.

players.name: Infosys
players.description: IT-services major; builder of Finacle core-banking product — proof that where India built the product, sovereignty followed.

players.name: Innovations for Defence Excellence
players.description: DDP startup/MSME procurement on-ramp; ADITI funds deep-tech incl. defence semiconductors and autonomy.

players.name: IREL (India) Limited
players.description: Sole domestic rare-earth miner/processor; monazite-to-NdPr and sole REE-magnet producer; single REE feedstock point.

players.name: Jio Platforms Limited
players.description: Reliance digital/telecom arm and IndiaAI-empanelled GPU provider; Google and Meta hold equity.

players.name: Kaynes Semicon Private Limited
players.description: Operates India's second operational OSAT (Sanand, Mar 2026); shipped first commercial multi-chip module.

players.name: Khanij Bidesh India Limited
players.description: NALCO/HCL/MECL JV; sole vehicle for sovereign offshore mineral acquisition (Argentina lithium, Australia partnership).

players.name: Krutrim
players.description: Ola-backed Indian AI/cloud platform building indigenous models and AI cloud infrastructure.

players.name: Krutrim SI Designs Private Limited
players.description: Ola-backed indigenous-model/cloud player; the non-state sovereign-model alternative to Sarvam.

players.name: Lam Research Corporation
players.description: Dominant etch/deposition equipment supplier; India ~100% import-dependent for etch tooling.

players.name: Larsen & Toubro Limited (Precision Engineering & Systems)
players.description: Largest private defence-engineering prime; K9 Vajra producer, AMCA consortium lead with BEL.

players.name: Lithium
players.description: Core battery metal; India ~100% import-dependent, refining China-routed; the defining import-dependency mineral.

players.name: Lohum Cleantech Private Limited
players.description: India's largest integrated Li-ion recycler; announced India's first battery-grade lithium refinery.

players.name: Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited
players.description: Premier submarine/destroyer yard; lead yard for the ~Rs 70,000 cr Project-75I submarine programme with TKMS.

players.name: Micron ATMP Sanand
players.description: First facility operational under ISM (Feb 2026); ~Rs 22,500 cr; first Made-in-India memory modules.

players.name: Micron Semiconductor Technology India Private Limited
players.description: Operates India's first operational chip unit (Sanand ATMP); DRAM/NAND assembly-test.

players.name: Microsoft Azure India
players.description: $17.5bn India commitment; anchor AdaniConneX tenant; primary foreign GPU-cloud for enterprise/government AI.

players.name: Microsoft Corporation
players.description: Productivity (M365), desktop OS (Windows), cloud (Azure) and ERP (Dynamics). Multi-layer dependency.

players.name: Mineral Exploration and Consultancy Limited
players.description: Miniratna drilling/exploration CPSE; converts GSI prospects into auctionable resources; co-owns KABIL.

players.name: Ministry of Defence (Government of India)
players.description: Apex authority owning the budget, DAC and DAP-2020 rules; sets indigenisation mandates routing value to domestic primes.

players.name: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
players.description: Parent ministry owning Semicon India, ISM, SPECS, DLI, SCL and IndiaAI Mission; the policy/budget chokepoint.

players.name: Ministry of Heavy Industries
players.description: Administers the rare-earth-magnet (REPM) and auto/ACC-battery PLI schemes; downstream-localisation policy owner.

players.name: Ministry of Mines
players.description: Apex mineral policymaker; amended MMDR Act 2023, owns KABIL, runs NCMM; the regulatory chokepoint.

players.name: Mishra Dhatu Nigam Limited
players.description: Sole Indian producer of titanium alloys and maraging steels; the materials chokepoint upstream of missiles, subs, engines.

players.name: MOSIP
players.description: Modular Open Source Identity Platform (IIIT Bangalore, 2018; Aadhaar model). Deployed in Philippines, Ethiopia, Morocco + advanced/pilot in 9+ countries; 100M+ IDs by 2023. India EXPORTS sovereign-identity software.

players.name: Munitions India Limited
players.description: Largest OFB-successor DPSU; core ammunition/explosives supplier — the war-stock/consumables base.

players.name: National Aluminium Company Limited
players.description: Integrated aluminium PSU; developing India's first gallium extraction (Damanjodi) and red-mud REE recovery; co-owns KABIL.

players.name: National Critical Mineral Mission
players.description: Rs 34,300 cr 7-year whole-of-value-chain mission: exploration, foreign assets, processing, recycling, stockpiling.

players.name: NTT Global Data Centers (India)
players.description: Largest colocation operator in India by share; ~500 MW Mumbai; IndiaAI second-tender qualified.

players.name: NVIDIA Corporation
players.description: THE chokepoint — ~80% of AI-GPU supply; every major Indian DC and the IndiaAI cluster run on Nvidia silicon.

players.name: Nxtra Data Limited
players.description: Bharti Airtel's DC subsidiary; couples compute capacity to Airtel's subsea backbone.

players.name: Oracle Corporation
players.description: ERP, database (market-leading) and cloud. Core foreign control of the database layer; owns FLEXCUBE core-banking (India-built).

players.name: People's Republic of China
players.description: Controls ~90% REE processing, ~98% gallium/germanium, ~90% battery-graphite refining; the binding chokepoint.

players.name: Perfios
players.description: Indian fintech SaaS (2024 unicorn); revenue ~Rs 558 cr FY24 (+37%).

players.name: Postman
players.description: Indian-founded API-development platform (global standard); now HQ overseas — emblem of the export-not-displace pattern.

players.name: Powerchip Semiconductor Manufacturing Corporation
players.description: Taiwanese foundry licensing the Dholera process technology; India's logic-fab process IP depends on PSMC.

players.name: Ramco Systems
players.description: Indian ERP vendor; among the few domestic players in the foreign-dominated ERP top tier.

players.name: Rare Earth Elements / Neodymium-Praseodymium
players.description: NdPr drives EV/wind/defence magnets; India ~100% China-dependent for finished NdFeB (imported 53,748 t FY25).

players.name: Reasi Lithium Deposit (Salal-Haimana, J&K)
players.description: India's headline 5.9 Mt lithium find; auction failed twice, returned to GSI — bellwether of non-bankable domestic supply.

players.name: Reliance Industries Limited
players.description: Largest single Indian AI-infra bet (~$110bn); GW-scale Jamnagar AI DC on Nvidia Blackwell with captive green power.

players.name: Renesas Electronics Corporation
players.description: Japanese IDM supplying packaging tech and anchor demand to CG Semi.

players.name: Russian Federation (Rosoboronexport / Almaz-Antey)
players.description: Largest source of India's legacy fleet and spares; S-400 and BrahMos JV partner.

players.name: Salesforce
players.description: CRM market leader; primary foreign competitor to Zoho/Freshworks in India.

players.name: SAP SE
players.description: Global ERP incumbent. >5,000 Indian enterprises run SAP; SAP India Pvt Ltd revenue ~Rs 8,870 cr FY25. Deepest-captured layer of India enterprise stack.

players.name: Sarvam AI
players.description: State-anointed sovereign-LLM builder; subsidised 4,096 H100s at Yotta; the services/IP node for model sovereignty.

players.name: Scheme for Promotion of Manufacturing of Electronic Components and Semiconductors
players.description: Capital-subsidy scheme for components/specialty materials; load-bearing for upstream localisation.

players.name: Scheme to Promote Manufacturing of Sintered Rare Earth Permanent Magnets
players.description: Rs 7,280 cr scheme (Nov 2025) to build 6,000 MTPA NdFeB magnet capacity; India's instrument to close the magnet import gap.

players.name: Semi-Conductor Laboratory
players.description: India's only operational government IDM fab (180nm); strategic chokepoint for ISRO/DRDO/defence-grade chips.

players.name: Semicon India Programme
players.description: Rs 76,000 cr umbrella scheme; up to 50% fiscal support that makes each approved unit viable.

players.name: ServiceNow
players.description: Enterprise workflow / ITSM platform; rising agentic-AI footprint.

players.name: SiCSem Private Limited
players.description: India's first commercial Silicon Carbide compound-semi fab (Bhubaneswar, with UK Clas-SiC); SiC power-device chokepoint.

players.name: Solar Industries India Limited
players.description: Largest private explosives/ammunition and loitering-munition maker (Nagastra, Pinaka rockets).

players.name: Specialty Semiconductor Gases and Photoresist
players.description: Photoresist, specialty gases, high-purity chemicals, wafers; >90% imported — the materials-layer chokepoint.

players.name: ST Telemedia Global Data Centres (India)
players.description: ~19% colocation share; $3.2bn / 550 MW India expansion; Singapore-state-linked top-three hosting capacity.

players.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company
players.description: ~70% global foundry share, >90% leading-edge; fabs all advanced AI silicon and India's DLI startup designs.

players.name: Tally Solutions
players.description: Dominant Indian SME accounting software; domestic anchor at the low end.

players.name: Tata Advanced Systems Limited
players.description: Leading private aerospace/defence prime; shortlisted for AMCA prototype, ATAGS co-producer, C-295 assembly.

players.name: Tata Communications Limited
players.description: IndiaAI-empanelled compute provider and major subsea/network operator within the Tata ecosystem.

players.name: Tata Consultancy Services
players.description: Largest Indian IT-services firm; BaNCS core-banking/financial products. Embodies the services-not-products path dependency.

players.name: Tata Electronics Private Limited
players.description: India's anchor fab/OSAT champion; operates the only approved domestic logic megafab and the Assam OSAT.

players.name: Tata Semiconductor Assembly and Test
players.description: India's largest indigenous OSAT (Jagiroad, Assam, ~Rs 27,000 cr); Phase 1 commissioning 2026.

players.name: Tata Semiconductor Fab, Dholera
players.description: India's first 300mm commercial logic fab (28-110nm, 50k WSPM, ~Rs 91,000 cr); the national fab chokepoint.

players.name: ThyssenKrupp Marine Systems
players.description: Design authority/technology partner for Project-75I submarines; supplies AIP and critical submarine tech.

players.name: Tokyo Electron Limited
players.description: ~90% share of photoresist coat/develop track systems; coat/develop chokepoint for every India fab.

players.name: Toyotsu Rare Earths India Private Limited
players.description: Toyota Tsusho subsidiary processing IREL rare earths; its 2025 export-pact suspension marks India's conservation pivot.

players.name: Vedanta Limited
players.description: Major private copper smelter/refiner (Tuticorin shut since 2018); REPM magnet bidder.

players.name: Wipro
players.description: IT-services major; early failed product attempt (Instaplan) illustrates the structural product gap.

players.name: Workday
players.description: Cloud HCM and finance; enterprise back-office dependency.

players.name: Yotta Data Services Private Limited
players.description: Most load-bearing IndiaAI GPU node — largest tender allocation and host of Sarvam's sovereign-model cluster.

players.name: Zoho Corporation
players.description: Bootstrapped Indian SaaS suite; revenue >Rs 12,000 cr FY25, profitable. Leading candidate for a sovereign enterprise-software suite.
